text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet town named Willowbrook, lived an unusual family: The Hendersons. They were peculiar, with their own language that only they could understand. Mrs. Henderson had the ability to speak several languages fluently; her son, Fredrick, could shape-shift; their daughter, Lily, had telekinetic powers; and their prized pet, Mr. Tibbles, could fly.

One bright morning, Mrs. Henderson brewed a batch of special herbal tea, promising it would wake them all up for their annual Spring Celebration. Fredrick changed into a bird just outside the house, gliding through the garden. Lily lifted petals into her mind, arranging them into colorful banners. And Mr. Tibbles buzzed around, looking mischievous.

Their neighbors were thrilled. Children eager for tales of Henderson magic; adults amused by the Henderson family's strangeness. They threw the most extravagant Spring Celebration the town had ever seen, complete with a parade, a tea party for
